---
format: html
title: interpolate_st_mev_to_grid()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>interpolate_st_mev_to_grid</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Interpolate 3-D Spatiotemporal MEVs to Prediction Grid</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Uses Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW, power = 2) in a
z-scored 3-D space '(x_km, y_km, age_kyr)' to approximate
spatiotemporal Moran Eigenvector Map (MEM) values from
training samples to prediction locations at a given age.
The interpolated values are then scaled using the training
spatial scale attributes.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>interpolate_st_mev_to_grid(
  data_st_mev_samples = NULL,
  data_coords_projected_train = NULL,
  data_coords_projected_pred = NULL,
  pred_age = NULL,
  spatial_scale_attributes = NULL
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data_st_mev_samples">data_st_mev_samples</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame with row names in the format
'&quot;&lt;dataset_name&gt;__&lt;age&gt;&quot;' and unscaled spatiotemporal MEV
columns ('mev_1', …), as returned by
'compute_spatiotemporal_mev()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_coords_projected_train">data_coords_projected_train</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame with 'coord_x_km' and 'coord_y_km' columns
and 'dataset_name' as row names (site level), as returned
by 'project_coords_to_metric()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_coords_projected_pred">data_coords_projected_pred</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame with 'coord_x_km' and 'coord_y_km' columns
and arbitrary row names identifying prediction locations,
as returned by 'project_coords_to_metric()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="pred_age">pred_age</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric or integer scalar. Age in years BP at which
predictions are made. Used as the temporal dimension of
the prediction 3-D coordinate matrix ('age_kyr =
pred_age / 1000').</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="spatial_scale_attributes">spatial_scale_attributes</code></td>
<td>
<p>Optional named list of '&quot;scaled:center&quot;' and '&quot;scaled:scale&quot;'
attributes per ST-MEV column, as returned by
'scale_spatial_for_fit()' in the 'spatial_scale_attributes'
element. When 'NULL', unscaled interpolated values are returned for
fold-local scaling.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>3-D coordinates '(x_km, y_km, age_kyr)' of the training
samples are z-scored using 'colMeans' and column-wise
standard deviations before computing Euclidean distances.
This ensures the spatial and temporal extents contribute
equally. The same z-score parameters are applied to the
prediction grid coordinates, fixing the temporal dimension
at 'pred_age / 1000'.
</p>
<p>This function handles the **3-D spatiotemporal case**.
For models fitted with 'spatial_mode = &quot;spatial&quot;' use
'interpolate_mev_to_grid()' instead.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A data frame with the same row names as
'data_coords_projected_pred' and one column per ST-MEV
(names matching 'data_st_mev_samples'). Columns are scaled when
'spatial_scale_attributes' is supplied and otherwise remain unscaled.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[compute_spatiotemporal_mev()],
[interpolate_mev_to_grid()],
[project_coords_to_metric()],
[scale_spatial_for_fit()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
